classification.reason: package.json with dependencies; src/ has 3+ source files; README has run instruction: 'npm ci'; Dockerfile present

executive_summary: Repository scores 68.0/100 — _functional_with_weakness_ band. Strongest: #01 Architectural coherence (4/5, weight 15%). Weakest: #09 Technical debt & risk (3/5, weight 5%). Highest-leverage improvement: Add structured logging and a /healthz route, and run lint+test in CI alongside the build.

rationale: Overall score 68.0 maps to stage=early_production per aggregation rules.

evidence.claim: Layered concept-based layout: data/store, utils (pure), config, components, routes — each with a single clear role
Pure utility modules (validation, integrity, format) hold all business logic, separated from rendering
Single reactive store owns all app data; routes and components read it rather than each fetching independently
Hand-written domain types are the single source of truth; comment forbids importing from internal modules
Dependency direction is clean: store imports utils; utils import only types; no utils->store or component->store-internals reaches

strengths.point: Store is the single reactive owner of state; components subscribe via $derived rather than duplicating data flow
Orchestrator pattern followed — page routes compose small components and call store/util functions, holding no rendering or business logic of their own
Validation and referential-integrity rules live in dedicated pure modules, not scattered through the store mutators

risks.point: CLAUDE.md prescribes a $lib/server/ services layer and +page.server.ts data fetching that this build does not implement, so the documented architecture does not match the actual one
A single global store singleton is the only data boundary; there is no service/adapter seam to swap localStorage for the documented Supabase backend without touching every mutator

up_one.condition: Introduce a documented data-access seam (e.g. $lib/server/ or a repository interface) so the store's localStorage backing can be swapped without editing mutators, matching CLAUDE.md's layering

down_one.condition: Let a component import store-internal persistence helpers directly, collapsing the store boundary

no-cross-layer-imports.claim: utils/ modules import only from $lib/types; the store imports utils and seed but no component imports the store's private persistence path

evidence.claim: svelte-check passes with 0 errors / 0 warnings across 4859 files
ESLint passes clean on all non-vendored source
Prettier reports all source files conform to code style
Store mutators are short, uniform, and return a discriminated SaveResult/DeleteResult rather than throwing — readable in isolation
Intent-revealing comments explain the why (e.g. why a plain Date, not SvelteDate, is used) rather than restating the code
Naming is consistent and descriptive across types, utils, and routes; no abbreviations or magical helpers

strengths.point: Result types (SaveResult, DeleteResult, DeleteCheck) give callers explicit success/failure handling instead of exceptions or nulls
Largest application components stay near the 150-line guideline; logic is extracted to utils and child components
Derived state is expressed declaratively with $derived chains rather than imperative recomputation

risks.point: The four explicit `any` escape hatches all sit in vendored shadcn chart/utils code, but they are tracked as source and would surface if that code is edited rather than treated as vendored
Filter state in the reports route uses terse single-letter prefixes (fProject, fAuthor) that read less clearly than the rest of the codebase

up_one.condition: Add a dedicated lint:src target that excludes vendored ui/ from any-density concerns and document the vendored boundary so the only escape hatches are provably upstream

down_one.condition: Introduce duplicated validation logic in a component instead of reusing $lib/utils/validation

evidence.claim: Multi-stage Dockerfile builds and runs the adapter-node server on port 3000
CI workflow runs type-check + build on every push/PR to dev and main
Dev and prod deploy workflows build/push the image and deploy to EC2 via Docker Compose + Caddy
.env.example documents the required runtime env vars with placeholders only
README has concrete run/build/deploy instructions and a full setup checklist
No structured logger, metrics, tracing, or health endpoint exists anywhere in the app

strengths.point: CI gates type-check and build on every push/PR, so a broken build cannot merge to dev or main
Dev/prod separation is real: distinct workflows, prefixed secrets, and separate containers behind Caddy auto-SSL
Secrets are kept entirely outside the repo — written to EC2 from GitHub Secrets at deploy time

risks.point: CI Build step injects Supabase/ORIGIN env stubs the app never reads, and the deploy workflows ship a frontend-only localStorage app while wiring it as if it had a Supabase backend — the operational story is template-inherited and decoupled from what runs
No health/readiness endpoint, structured logging, env validation at startup, or graceful shutdown — failures surface only as raw adapter-node crashes
CI does not run the test suite or lint — only type-check and build — so the 35 tests are not enforced before merge

up_one.condition: Add structured logging and a /healthz route, and run lint+test in CI alongside the build

down_one.condition: Remove the Dockerfile or break the CI build step

no-observability-hooks.claim: No health endpoint, metrics, tracing, structured logger, or startup env validation exists in src/ — the app has no server routes at all (no hooks.server.ts, no +server.ts)

evidence.claim: TypeScript strict mode is enabled and checkJs is on
svelte-check passes with zero type errors across the project
Untrusted all-string Draft shapes are validated at the store boundary into typed domain objects before entering state
Validation re-checks enum membership and referential existence at the boundary, not just presence — e.g. role/status/owner must be valid and exist
Domain enums are const-tuple-derived union types, so invalid literals are unrepresentable in typed code

strengths.point: Clean Draft -> validate -> typed-domain pipeline treats form input as tainted until validated, exactly the contract discipline the rubric rewards
Const-assertion enums give compile-time exhaustiveness — overallStatusTone's switch has no default and still type-checks

risks.point: The only explicit `any` usages are in vendored shadcn chart code, not authored boundary code, but they are committed as source
Validation casts draft strings with `as Role`/`as ProjectStatus` after an includes() check; correct here, but the cast pattern is easy to misuse if a future field skips the guard

up_one.condition: Add a schema library (Zod/Valibot) so the Draft->domain mapping is a single typed parse with inferred types instead of manual includes()+cast pairs

evidence.claim: vitest is configured and the test suite runs green: 4 files, 35 tests passing
Store tests cover CRUD, referential-integrity blocking, and timestamp semantics with an injected deterministic clock
Tests exist beyond the inherited example — store, validation, and integrity logic are unit-tested
Validation edge cases are tested (invalid email, missing fields, nonexistent references)

strengths.point: Tests assert real behavior on the business-logic core (referential integrity, create/update/delete invariants), not trivial getters
Deterministic clock injection makes timestamp assertions stable and non-flaky

risks.point: No component, route, or end-to-end tests — the entire UI layer (forms, dialogs, tables, dashboard derivations) is untested; only pure logic and the store are covered
CI never runs the test suite (only type-check + build), so a regression in tested logic would not block a merge

up_one.condition: Run vitest in CI on every PR and add a few @testing-library/svelte component tests for the form dialogs

down_one.condition: Remove the vitest config and test script

unit-only-no-component-or-e2e.claim: All four test files target pure functions or the store class; no .svelte component tests and no Playwright/e2e harness exist

scalability_extensibility.confidence_reason: Surface area is small and frontend-only; extensibility is judged on the present seams (store mutators, config-driven nav, feature-grouped components) rather than a proven scaling path.

evidence.claim: Navigation is config-driven — adding a section/page is a data edit, not a component rewrite
Components are grouped by feature (reports/, projects/, users/), so a new entity can follow the existing slice pattern
Adding a new domain entity means a uniform set of edits (type + validate + store mutator + route), each in an isolated module
No N+1 or unbounded server loops are possible — all data is in-memory arrays filtered/derived client-side

strengths.point: Per-feature component directories let multiple engineers work on users/projects/reports in parallel with minimal collision
The store concentrates all mutation in one well-tested class, so cross-cutting changes (e.g. swapping the backing store) have a single edit point

risks.point: Every read filters the full in-memory array on each access (e.g. userById is a linear find called per table row); fine at seed scale, but there is no indexing or pagination seam for growth
localStorage persistence rewrites the entire dataset on every mutation and has no migration story, so the backing store cannot scale or evolve schema without a rewrite

up_one.condition: Introduce the documented service/repository seam so the store can move to a paginated backend without touching consumers, and add the o(1) lookup maps the per-row finds want

down_one.condition: Add a god-file central registry that every new feature must edit

in-memory-no-query-layer.claim: There is no database or query layer; all entities are $state arrays filtered and derived client-side, so server-side N+1 / blocking-I/O footguns do not apply

evidence.claim: No hardcoded secrets, API keys, or private keys in source
No eval or new Function on any input
.env.example contains placeholders only and is explicitly marked reference-only
All external input (form drafts) is validated at the store boundary before mutating state; failures return typed errors rather than crashing
Errors propagate explicitly via Result types; the one broad catch (JSON.parse of localStorage) falls back to reset() deliberately, not silently
There is no server, SQL, shell-out, or deserialization of untrusted remote data — the only trust boundary is form input into the client store

strengths.point: Secrets are fully externalized — none in source, deploy injects them from GitHub Secrets at runtime
Input validation is centralized and re-checks referential existence, so a forged draft referencing a nonexistent owner/project is rejected

risks.point: README and CLAUDE.md claim a (app)/+layout.ts auth guard that redirects unauthenticated users to /login, but neither that load function nor a /login route exists — the protected route group renders unconditionally with no auth

CLAUDE.md asserts RLS-and-Azure-OAuth as the security model, but the shipped app has no auth, no Supabase, and no server boundary at all — the documented threat model does not describe the running code
localStorage is the data store; any XSS or shared-machine access can read/modify all app data, with no integrity protection

up_one.condition: Either implement the documented auth guard + /login or correct the docs to describe the actual no-auth client-only model, removing the phantom trust boundary

down_one.condition: Commit a real secret to source or introduce a server endpoint that interpolates form input into a query/shell command

no-server-trust-boundary.claim: The app has no server routes, hooks, SQL, or shell execution; the sole external-input path is form drafts entering the client-side store

evidence.claim: README documents stack, quick start, setup checklist, scripts, and project structure
CLAUDE.md plus a 17KB STYLEGUIDE.md provide architecture and coding conventions
ESLint flat config is present, thoughtfully tuned, and passes clean; vendored ui/ and the styleguide are ignored with rationale comments
Prettier config + ignore are committed and source conforms
package-lock.json is committed and dependencies are version-pinned with carets
Repo layout is clean and conventional: src/lib by role, infra/, supabase/, .github/workflows

strengths.point: Lint config is deliberate, not boilerplate — it disables a SvelteKit rule that conflicts with the config-driven nav pattern and documents why
Commit subjects follow conventional style with meaningful intent (feat: / chore:), not 'wip'/'fix' dumps

risks.point: Documentation describes a different application than the one that exists: README's Project Structure and Auth Architecture sections reference hooks.server.ts, login/, auth/callback/, and +layout.server.ts that are absent — a reader following the docs is misled
CI does not enforce lint or tests (only type-check + build), so the committed lint/test discipline is not gated
Only two commits exist (template scaffold + one MVP commit), so there is no incremental history to demonstrate sustained discipline

up_one.condition: Run lint+test in CI and reconcile README/CLAUDE.md with the actual frontend-only build (or add the documented backend)

down_one.condition: Remove the ESLint config or stop committing the lockfile

conventional-repo-layout.claim: Top-level layout is conventional and self-explanatory: src/lib organized by role (data, utils, config, components), plus infra/, supabase/, .github/workflows, and committed config files

two-commit-history.claim: git log shows exactly two commits on the branch: a template scaffold and a single MVP feature commit

evidence.claim: Zero TODO/FIXME/HACK/XXX markers anywhere in src/
The single eslint-disable in authored code is justified by an inline rationale, not a silent suppression
No competing patterns: one store, one validation module, one toast store — no drift between coexisting approaches
Largest hidden risk is documentation that describes a backend (Supabase/auth/RLS/server) the build does not contain

strengths.point: Effectively zero declared debt at this size — no TODO markers, no commented-out code, no dead scaffolding in authored modules
Brittle-seam comments are honest: the store flags exactly why deleteReport is unconditional and why a plain Date is used

risks.point: The biggest 12-month risk is the doc/reality gap: README+CLAUDE.md+.env.example+CI all assume a Supabase/auth backend, so the next engineer will either wire phantom infrastructure or distrust the docs — silent architectural drift baked in at scaffold time
localStorage-as-database with full-rewrite persistence and no migration path is acceptable for an MVP but is real debt the moment data must survive a browser or be shared

up_one.condition: Reconcile the docs with the actual build (or implement the backend) so the documented architecture stops being latent debt

down_one.condition: Introduce untracked TODO/FIXME markers in hot paths

doc-reality-drift.claim: README, CLAUDE.md, .env.example, and the CI build step all describe/assume a Supabase + Azure-OAuth + server-hooks backend, but the shipped app is frontend-only with a localStorage store and no server routes, auth, or Supabase usage

top_strengths.point: Store is the single reactive owner of state; components subscribe via $derived rather than duplicating data flow
Result types (SaveResult, DeleteResult, DeleteCheck) give callers explicit success/failure handling instead of exceptions or nulls
CI gates type-check and build on every push/PR, so a broken build cannot merge to dev or main
Clean Draft -> validate -> typed-domain pipeline treats form input as tainted until validated, exactly the contract discipline the rubric rewards
Tests assert real behavior on the business-logic core (referential integrity, create/update/delete invariants), not trivial getters

top_risks.point: CI Build step injects Supabase/ORIGIN env stubs the app never reads, and the deploy workflows ship a frontend-only localStorage app while wiring it as if it had a Supabase backend — the operational story is template-inherited and decoupled from what runs
No component, route, or end-to-end tests — the entire UI layer (forms, dialogs, tables, dashboard derivations) is untested; only pure logic and the store are covered
Every read filters the full in-memory array on each access (e.g. userById is a linear find called per table row); fine at seed scale, but there is no indexing or pagination seam for growth

README and CLAUDE.md claim a (app)/+layout.ts auth guard that redirects unauthenticated users to /login, but neither that load function nor a /login route exists — the protected route group renders unconditionally with no auth

Documentation describes a different application than the one that exists: README's Project Structure and Auth Architecture sections reference hooks.server.ts, login/, auth/callback/, and +layout.server.ts that are absent — a reader following the docs is misled
